data.change_summary: Ninth remediation round addressing 3 QA amendments: (1) Added a guard in backoff_for_attempt to return Duration::ZERO when attempt is 0, preventing a u32 underflow from saturating_sub(1) that would produce the maximum backoff (60s cap) instead of zero. (2) Fixed tmux adapter hardcoding FailureClass::TransportFailure for all non-zero exits: made classify_exit_failure pub(crate) in process_backend.rs and imported it in tmux.rs, so exit code 127 is now classified as BinaryNotFound (terminal) in tmux mode, matching the acceptance criteria. (3) Added inner timeout enforcement to TmuxAdapter::invoke() by wrapping wait_for_session_exit in tokio::time::timeout(request.timeout, ...). On timeout, the adapter calls self.cancel() for graceful SIGTERM → SIGKILL cleanup, then calls preserve_failed_artifacts to move temp files to runtime/failed before returning InvocationFailed{Timeout}. Re-enabled enforces_timeout() returning true for TmuxAdapter since it now enforces its own deadline, so the service adds the 30s safety-net buffer.
outstanding_risks: Process group kill cannot be implemented due to #![forbid(unsafe_code)] — pre_exec(setsid) requires unsafe, and std::process::Command::process_group is nightly-only. Grandchildren spawned by claude/codex CLI may survive as orphans after the parent is killed on timeout/cancel.

The BinaryNotFound failure class name is used for both missing binaries and missing API keys. Doc comments clarify this dual use as a general 'fatal infrastructure prerequisite' signal, but operator logs will still show 'binary_not_found' for missing env vars.

The pre-existing flaky test service_emits_invocation_completed_trace_with_token_fields occasionally fails under high parallelism due to tracing subscriber interference between tests. This is unrelated to our changes and passes reliably in isolation.

steps.description: Added attempt == 0 guard to backoff_for_attempt in retry_policy.rs: if attempt is 0, return Duration::ZERO immediately. This prevents the u32 underflow from saturating_sub(1) which would produce u32::MAX as the exponent, saturating the multiplier and resulting in 60s (the cap) instead of the expected 0s for a nonsensical attempt number.

Made classify_exit_failure pub(crate) in process_backend.rs and imported it in tmux.rs. Updated the tmux adapter's exit-failure path (tmux.rs:581) to call classify_exit_failure(output.status) instead of hardcoding FailureClass::TransportFailure. Exit code 127 is now classified as BinaryNotFound (terminal) in tmux mode, matching the process backend behavior.

Added inner timeout enforcement to TmuxAdapter::invoke(): wrapped wait_for_session_exit() in tokio::time::timeout(request.timeout, ...). On timeout, the handler calls self.cancel() for graceful SIGTERM → grace period → SIGKILL shutdown (matching the existing cancel flow), then calls prepared.preserve_failed_artifacts() to move Codex temp files to runtime/failed, and returns InvocationFailed{Timeout}. Re-enabled enforces_timeout() returning true for TmuxAdapter so the service adds the 30s safety-net buffer.

Verified all 678 tests pass (cargo test), cargo clippy -- -D warnings clean, cargo fmt --check clean. Feature-gated: all 7 tmux_adapter_test tests pass (including tmux_adapter_timeout_cleans_up_session_with_sigterm_then_sigkill), service_times_out_and_requests_backend_cancellation passes, all 5 retry_policy_test tests pass.

validation_evidence: cargo test: 678 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored across all test binaries
cargo clippy -- -D warnings: no warnings or errors

tmux_adapter_timeout_cleans_up_session_with_sigterm_then_sigkill: passes — adapter-level timeout at request.timeout fires, cancel() sends SIGTERM then SIGKILL, preserve_failed_artifacts preserves temp files, service promotes InvocationFailed{Timeout} to InvocationTimeout
All 7 tmux_adapter_test tests pass: timeout, cancel with SIGTERM/SIGKILL, direct output match, session name determinism, availability check, and openrouter rejection
service_times_out_and_requests_backend_cancellation (--features test-stub): passes — stub adapter still uses service-level timeout (enforces_timeout() returns false)

All 5 retry_policy_test tests pass: default limits, backoff exponential growth capped at 60s, no_backoff returns zero — the attempt == 0 guard adds no regressions since the existing tests use 1-indexed attempts
classify_exit_failure is now shared between ProcessBackendAdapter and TmuxAdapter via pub(crate) visibility — exit 127 produces BinaryNotFound in both execution modes
